2605.21097.title: WCXB: A Multi-Type Web Content Extraction Benchmark
2605.21097.paper_summary: Web content extraction - isolating a page's main content from surrounding boilerplate - is a prerequisite for search indexing, retrieval-augmented generation, NLP dataset construction, and large language model training. Progress in this area has been constrained by the limitations of existing evaluation benchmarks, which are small (100-800 pages), restricted to news articles, or based on web pages from over a decade ago. We introduce the Web Content Extraction Benchmark (WCXB), a dataset of 2,008 web pages from 1,613 domains spanning seven structurally distinct page types: articles, forums, products, collections, listings, documentation, and service pages. The dataset includes a 1,497-page development set and a 511-page held-out test set with matched page type distributions. Ground truth annotations were produced through a five-stage pipeline: LLM-assisted drafting, automated verification, four-pass frontier model review, snippet and quality verification scripts, and human review. We evaluate 13 extraction systems - 11 heuristic and 2 neural - and find that while top systems converge on articles (F1 = 0.93), performance diverges sharply on structured page types (F1 = 0.41-0.84), revealing blind spots invisible to existing article-only benchmarks. The dataset is released under CC-BY-4.0 with HTML source files, ground truth annotations, page type labels, and baseline results.
2605.21097.paper_summary_zh: 網頁內容提取 - 從周圍的模板中孤立出頁面的主要內容 - 是搜索索引、檢索增強生成、NLP 數據集構建和大型語言模型訓練的先決條件。這一領域的進展受到現有評估基準的限制，這些基準要麼規模小（100-800 頁），要麼僅限於新聞文章，或者基於十多年前的網頁。我們介紹了網頁內容提取基準（WCXB），這是一個包含 2,008 個來自 1,613 個域的網頁數據集，涵蓋七種結構上不同的頁面類型：文章、論壇、產品、集合、列表、文檔和服務頁面。該數據集包括一個 1,497 頁的開發集和一個 511 頁的保留測試集，並具有匹配的頁面類型分佈。真實標註是通過五個階段的管道產生的：LLM 輔助草擬、自動驗證、四次通過的邊界模型審查、片段和質量驗證腳本，以及人工審查。我們評估了 13 個提取系統 - 11 個啟發式和 2 個神經網絡 - 發現雖然頂尖系統在文章上的表現趨於一致（F1 = 0.93），但在結構化頁面類型上的表現卻大相徑庭（F1 = 0.41-0.84），揭示了現有僅限於文章的基準無法察覺的盲點。該數據集以 CC-BY-4.0 授權發布，包含 HTML 源文件、真實標註、頁面類型標籤和基準結果。
2605.21097.author: Murrough Foley et.al.
